Fig. 3: Qualitative comparison on the SelfCap 1200-frame scenes.
Table 1: Quantitative comparison on the SelfCap dataset across 600, 900, and 1200

Fig. 2: Overview of our framework. We comprehensively model the scene dynamics through three key components. First, the motion is parameterized via a hierarchical decomposition using Bézier residuals within an effective temporal window. Second, this Lie algebra representation is mapped to a coupled SE(3) transformation. Third, the transformation is applied relative to a gauge-fixed local anchor (ai,⊥) to ensure stable, articulate deformation. Finally, the deformed primitives are rendered to optimize both photometric and motion regularization objectives.
